overview: Add `viewnode ns get-current` and `viewnode ns set-current` to mirror the existing `ctx` workflow, respect the root `--kubeconfig` flag, validate namespace changes against the current cluster before persisting them, cover the new behavior with tests, and update the manually maintained README examples.

userStories.title: Wire namespace current-state subcommands
userStories.description: Extend the existing `ns` Cobra command group with `get-current` and `set-current` subcommands so namespace current-state operations have the same entry-point structure and alias pattern as the existing `ctx` command family.
acceptanceCriteria: The `ns` command registers `get-current` and `set-current` subcommands without changing existing `ns list` behavior.
The new subcommands include the legacy-style aliases `getcurrent` and `setcurrent`.
The command definitions follow the existing `cmd/ctx` structural pattern so future namespace operations remain in the same command tree.
userStories.notes: Reuse the established Cobra command organization in `cmd/ns` and avoid introducing any parallel command structure.

userStories.title: Implement namespace current lookup
userStories.description: Add `viewnode ns get-current` to read the current namespace from the active kubeconfig context using the root-command config initialization flow, and preserve Kubernetes default behavior when no namespace is explicitly set.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `viewnode ns get-current` prints the current namespace from the active kubeconfig context followed by a trailing newline.
If the active kubeconfig context does not define a namespace, the command prints `default`.
The command resolves kubeconfig through the same root-command flow as the rest of the CLI, including support for the inherited `--kubeconfig` flag.
Failures while initializing config or reading kubeconfig state are returned as command errors.
userStories.notes: Initialize config from `cmd.Root()` with fallback to the current command so inherited persistent flags resolve correctly.

userStories.title: Implement validated namespace current update
userStories.description: Add `viewnode ns set-current <namespace>` to validate the requested namespace against the current cluster, then update only the active kubeconfig context namespace and report a clear success or failure result.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `viewnode ns set-current <namespace>` with an existing namespace updates only the active kubeconfig context namespace.
On success, the command prints `current namespace set to <namespace>`.
The command validates that the requested namespace exists in the current cluster before writing kubeconfig.
If the requested namespace does not exist, the command returns a descriptive error and does not mutate kubeconfig.
If kubeconfig read or write operations fail, the command returns a descriptive error instead of silently succeeding.
The command respects the inherited root `--kubeconfig` flag.
userStories.notes: Keep cluster validation and kubeconfig mutation separate so failed validation cannot accidentally persist a change.

userStories.title: Verify and document namespace current-state commands
userStories.description: Add automated coverage for the new namespace commands, including inherited flag behavior, and update the manually maintained README command reference and examples to reflect the new user-facing commands and aliases.
acceptanceCriteria: Automated tests cover `ns get-current` success, `default` fallback, and inherited `--kubeconfig` propagation through a small root command tree executed with `Execute()`.
Automated tests cover `ns set-current` success, persisted kubeconfig mutation, missing-namespace validation failure, and at least one kubeconfig error path.
The namespace command section in `README.md` documents `ns get-current`, `ns set-current`, and the `getcurrent` and `setcurrent` aliases.
userStories.notes: Follow the repository testing convention for Cobra inherited flags and keep README command listings in sync in the same change.
